cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at this question. The passage is about a new building in the South Bronx called Fox Point, which is a 47-unit housing project. It's clean and cool, using special equipment to produce energy and reuse heat, which reduces carbon emissions and costs. The building is operated by Palladia, and they got support from ECP (Enterprise Community Partners), which has Green Communities Standards. These standards focus on healthy, environmentally friendly, and affordable homes. The standards increase construction costs by 2%, but they're paid back through lower operating costs. The city's mayor, Michael Bloomberg, wants to create 165,000 affordable housing units for half a million people. The passage mentions that about 80% of New York's greenhouse gas emissions come from buildings, and 40% of those are from housing. The city's DHPD will require all new projects to follow ECP's green standards. Other cities like Cleveland and Denver have similar measures, but New York's DHPD is the largest city developer of affordable housing in the country. Now the question is asking what can be learned from the text. The options are: A) New York City is seriously polluted B) people's daily life causes many carbon emissions in New York City C) a great number of people in New York City don't have houses to live in D) some other cities have developed more affordable housing than New York City Let me break down the options. First, option A: "New York City is seriously polluted." The passage does mention that buildings contribute a lot to greenhouse gas emissions (80%), and housing is a part of that (40% of the 80%).
